NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. With stringed instruments. A Contemplation of David when the Ziphites went and said to Saul, “Is David not hiding with us?” Save me, O God, by Your name, And vindicate me by Your strength.
NKJV.2: Hear my prayer, O God; Give ear to the words of my mouth.
NKJV.3: For strangers have risen up against me, And oppressors have sought after my life; They have not set God before them. Selah
NKJV.4: Behold, God is my helper; The Lord is with those who uphold my life.
NKJV.5: He will repay my enemies for their evil. Cut them off in Your truth.
NKJV.6: I will freely sacrifice to You; I will praise Your name, O LORD, for it is good.
NKJV.7: For He has delivered me out of all trouble; And my eye has seen its desire upon my enemies.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> on Neginoth<S>5058</S>, Maschil<S>4905</S>, A Psalm of David<S>1732</S>, when the Ziphims<S>2130</S> came<S>935</S> and said<S>559</S> to Saul<S>7586</S>, Doth not David<S>1732</S> hide<S>5641</S> himself with us? Save<S>3467</S> me, O God<S>430</S>, by thy name<S>8034</S>, and judge<S>1777</S> me by thy strength<S>1369</S>.
KJV.2: Hear<S>8085</S> my prayer<S>8605</S>, O God<S>430</S>; give ear<S>238</S> to the words<S>561</S> of my mouth<S>6310</S>.
KJV.3: For strangers<S>2114</S> are risen up<S>6965</S> against me, and oppressors<S>6184</S> seek<S>1245</S> after my soul<S>5315</S>: they have not set<S>7760</S> God<S>430</S> before them. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.4: Behold, God<S>430</S> is mine helper<S>5826</S>: the Lord<S>136</S> is with them that uphold<S>5564</S> my soul<S>5315</S>.
KJV.5: He shall reward<S>7725</S> <S>7725</S> evil<S>7451</S> unto mine enemies<S>8324</S>: cut them off<S>6789</S> in thy truth<S>571</S>. <sup>mine: Heb. those that observe me</sup>
KJV.6: I will freely<S>5071</S> sacrifice<S>2076</S> unto thee: I will praise<S>3034</S> thy name<S>8034</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>; for it is good<S>2896</S>.
KJV.7: For he hath delivered<S>5337</S> me out of all trouble<S>6869</S>: and mine eye<S>5869</S> hath seen<S>7200</S> his desire upon mine enemies<S>341</S>.

ESV.1: O God, save me by your name, and vindicate me by your might.
ESV.2: O God, hear my prayer; give ear to the words of my mouth.
ESV.3: For strangers have risen against me; ruthless men seek my life; they do not set God before themselves. Selah
ESV.4: Behold, God is my helper; the Lord is the upholder of my life.
ESV.5: He will return the evil to my enemies; in your faithfulness put an end to them.
ESV.6: With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to you; I will give thanks to your name, O LORD, for it is good.
ESV.7: For he has delivered me from every trouble, and my eye has looked in triumph on my enemies.

NASB.1: For the choir director; on stringed instruments. A Maskil of David, when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, 'Is not David hiding himself among us?' Save me, O God, by Your name, And vindicate me by Your power.
NASB.2: Hear my prayer, O God; Give ear to the words of my mouth.
NASB.3: For strangers have risen against me And violent men have sought my life; They have not set God before them. Selah.
NASB.4: Behold, God is my helper; The Lord is the sustainer of my soul.
NASB.5: He will recompense the evil to my foes; Destroy them in Your faithfulness.
NASB.6: Willingly I will sacrifice to You; I will give thanks to Your name, O LORD, for it is good.
NASB.7: For He has delivered me from all trouble, And my eye has looked [with satisfaction] upon my enemies.

NLT.1:
Come with great power, O God, and rescue me!
Defend me with your might.
NLT.2:
Listen to my prayer, O God.
Pay attention to my plea.
NLT.3:
For strangers are attacking me;
violent people are trying to kill me.
They care nothing for God. Interlude
NLT.4:
But God is my helper.
The Lord keeps me alive!
NLT.5:
May the evil plans of my enemies be turned against them.
Do as you promised and put an end to them.
NLT.6:
I will sacrifice a voluntary offering to you;
I will praise your name, O LORD,
for it is good.
NLT.7:
For you have rescued me from my troubles
and helped me to triumph over my enemies.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. On stringed instruments. A contemplation by David, when the Ziphites came and said to Saul, “Isn’t David hiding himself among us?” Save me, God, by your name. Vindicate me in your might.
WEB.2: Hear my prayer, God. Listen to the words of my mouth.
WEB.3: For strangers have risen up against me. Violent men have sought after my soul. They haven’t set God before them. Selah.
WEB.4: Behold, God is my helper. The Lord is the one who sustains my soul.
WEB.5: He will repay the evil to my enemies. Destroy them in your truth.
WEB.6: With a free will offering, I will sacrifice to you. I will give thanks to your name, Yahweh, for it is good.
WEB.7: For he has delivered me out of all trouble. My eye has seen triumph over my enemies.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: with stringed instruments. A of David. When the Ziphites went and said to Saul, “Is David not hiding among us?” ⓐ [1] God, save me by your name,
and vindicate me by your might! ⓑ
CSB17.2:
God, hear my prayer;
listen to the words from my mouth. ⓒ
CSB17.3:
For strangers rise up against me,
and violent men intend to kill me.
They do not let God guide them. [1] ⓓ Selah
CSB17.4:
God is my helper;
the Lord is the sustainer of my life. [2] ⓔ
CSB17.5:
He will repay my adversaries for their evil.
Because of your faithfulness, annihilate them. ⓕ
CSB17.6:
I will sacrifice a freewill offering to you. ⓖ
I will praise your name, LORD,
because it is good. ⓗ
CSB17.7:
For he has rescued me from every trouble,
and my eye has looked down on my enemies. ⓘ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; with stringed instruments. A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem, of David, when the Ziphites went and told Saul, "David is hiding among us." Save me, O God, by Your name;
And vindicate me by Your [wondrous] power.
AMP.2:
Hear my prayer, O God;
Listen to the words of my mouth.
AMP.3:
For strangers have risen against me
And violent men have sought my life;
They have not set God before them. Selah.
AMP.4:
Behold, God is my helper and ally;
The Lord is the sustainer of my soul [my upholder].
AMP.5:
He will pay back the evil to my enemies;
In Your faithfulness destroy them.
AMP.6:
With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to You;
I will give thanks and praise Your name, O LORD, for it is good.
AMP.7:
For He has rescued me from every trouble,
And my eye has looked with satisfaction (triumph) on my enemies.

MSG.1:
A David psalm, when the Ziphites reported to Saul, "David is hiding out with us."
God, for your sake, help me!
Use your influence to clear me.
MSG.2:
Listen, God—I'm desperate.
Don't be too busy to hear me.
MSG.3:
Outlaws are out to get me,
hit men are trying to kill me.
Nothing will stop them;
God means nothing to them.
MSG.4:
Oh, look! God's right here helping!
God's on my side,
MSG.5:
Evil is looping back on my enemies.
Don't let up! Finish them off!
MSG.6:
I'm ready now to worship, so ready.
I thank you, God—you're so good.
MSG.7:
You got me out of every scrape,
and I saw my enemies get it.

NIV.1:
Psalm 54
For the director of music. With stringed instruments. A maskil of David. When the Ziphites had gone to Saul and said, “Is not David hiding among us?”
Save me, O God, by your name;
vindicate me by your might.
NIV.2:
Hear my prayer, O God;
listen to the words of my mouth.
NIV.3:
Strangers are attacking me;
ruthless men seek my life—
men without regard for God. Selah
NIV.4:
Surely God is my help;
the Lord is the one who sustains me.
NIV.5:
Let evil recoil on those who slander me;
in your faithfulness destroy them.
NIV.6:
I will sacrifice a freewill offering to you;
I will praise your name, O Lord,
for it is good.
NIV.7:
For he has delivered me from all my troubles,
and my eyes have looked in triumph on my foes.

NRSVCE.1:
Save me, O God, by your name,
and vindicate me by your might.
NRSVCE.2:
Hear my prayer, O God;
give ear to the words of my mouth.
NRSVCE.3:
For the insolent have risen against me,
the ruthless seek my life;
they do not set God before them. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
But surely, God is my helper;
the Lord is the upholder of my life.
NRSVCE.5:
He will repay my enemies for their evil.
In your faithfulness, put an end to them.
NRSVCE.6:
With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to you;
I will give thanks to your name, O LORD, for it is good.
NRSVCE.7:
For he has delivered me from every trouble,
and my eye has looked in triumph on my enemies.

NET.1: O God, deliver me by your name! Vindicate me by your power!
NET.2: O God, listen to my prayer! Pay attention to what I say!
NET.3: For foreigners attack me; ruthless men, who do not respect God, seek my life. (Selah)
NET.4: Look, God is my deliverer! The Lord is among those who support me.
NET.5: May those who wait to ambush me be repaid for their evil! As a demonstration of your faithfulness, destroy them!
NET.6: With a freewill offering I will sacrifice to you! I will give thanks to your name, O LORD, for it is good!
NET.7: Surely he rescues me from all trouble, and I triumph over my enemies.
